title: Dependent Azure Resources
summary: Dependent Azure resources are supporting services used by Foundry or hub-based projects, including Azure Storage, Azure Key Vault, Azure Container Registry, Application Insights, Log Analytics, Azure AI Search, and the Foundry resource itself depending on project shape.
analogy: A project is the classroom; dependent resources are the lockers, safe, workshop, camera system, and library index that make the classroom usable.
diagram:
graph TD
Foundry[Foundry or hub project] --> Store[Storage account]
Foundry --> Vault[Key Vault]
Foundry --> Registry[Container Registry]
Foundry --> Monitor[Application Insights]
Foundry --> Logs[Log Analytics]
Foundry --> Search[Azure AI Search]
Store --> BoundaryA[Separate governance]
Vault --> BoundaryB[Separate governance]
body:
## First principles

First principles: a platform feature often relies on ordinary infrastructure underneath. Files need storage, secrets need a vault, images may need a registry, logs need monitoring, and retrieval may need search. Foundry hides some wiring, but architects must still understand the dependent resources and their separate governance boundaries.

## Definition

Dependent Azure resources are supporting services used by Foundry or hub-based projects, including Azure Storage, Azure Key Vault, Azure Container Registry, Application Insights, Log Analytics, Azure AI Search, and the Foundry resource itself depending on project shape.

## How it fits together

Do not assume connected or dependent resources inherit every setting automatically. The architecture docs state that connected resources such as Storage, Key Vault, and Azure AI Search are independent Azure resources with their own governance boundaries, networking, and access policies.

## Worked example

```bash
# Shows resource types so you can spot storage, vault, registry, insights, logs, and search.
# Requires Azure RBAC read access. No keys or secrets are displayed.
az resource list   --resource-group <resource-group>   --query "[].{name:name,type:type,location:location}"   -o table
```

*Inventory supporting resources in a resource group*

drills.q: Why should a learner care about dependent resources?
drills.a: Because cost, networking, RBAC, and compliance can live on those resources separately from Foundry.
Azure Storage and Azure Key Vault.

drills.q: Are connected resources always governed by the Foundry resource?
drills.a: No. They are independent Azure resources with their own governance boundaries.

teachItBack: Foundry feels unified, but it can depend on separate Azure resources. Storage, Key Vault, Container Registry, Application Insights, Log Analytics, and Search may all matter. Teach peers to inspect the whole resource group and govern each dependency deliberately.

glossary.definition: Cloud storage service often used for project artifacts and files.
Azure service for securely storing secrets, keys, and certificates.
Registry for container images used by some custom runtime scenarios.
Azure Monitor log workspace for querying telemetry.
